concept: Dose / Specified Amount of an Intervention at a Stated Administration
description: One specified amount given at one administration. A dose carries no unit, route, or schedule of its own; those live in the exact source instruction, precise in a way Phi's grammar is not built to approximate.
sound_symbolism: Closed lips give 'mi' a small nasal opening that rises into bright 'i'. Stressed 'ka' shifts the closure back to the velum and releases it through broad 'a'. Final 'su' draws the air into a hiss and drops the vowel into deep 'u'. Three compact contacts give the word a measured pulse.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia ha mikasu reo ma shelomu.' (I do not understand the meaning of this dose.) Preserve exact amount, unit, route, timing, formulation, and source instruction outside Phi. Distinct from 'nepha' (medicine), 'nomiki' (concentration), 'thimora' (schedule), 'masue' (measure), and total treatment.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating a dose from its exact record, safety, understanding, and consent prevents a number or instruction from speaking as unquestionable authority.

semantic_domains.cognition: a specified intervention amount requiring exact source interpretation
semantic_domains.physical: an amount intended or delivered to a body
semantic_domains.temporal: an amount tied to an administration or interval
